root_cause: HPA 'api-hpa' is thrashing the workload by repeatedly reversing replica recommendations
evidence: HPA rescale direction reversed 3 times over 17.0 minutes
ReplicaSet 'api-6dbf78c7f9' was alternately scaled as: up to 6 -> down to 4 -> up to 6 -> down to 4
Deployment 'api' still reports only 2/4 available replicas and 2/4 updated replicas
HPA behavior disables downscale damping with scaleDown.stabilizationWindowSeconds=0
SuccessfulRescale reasons flipped between opposing scaling signals within the same incident window

causes.message: Timeline shows HPA 'api-hpa' repeatedly rescaling Deployment/api
HorizontalPodAutoscaler keeps reversing desired replica recommendations instead of converging on a stable size
ReplicaSet 'api-6dbf78c7f9' keeps getting scaled up and down in response to those HPA decisions
HPA behavior reduces or disables downscale damping, which makes flapping replica recommendations more likely
Deployment availability remains unstable at 2/4 available replicas while autoscaling keeps reversing

pod:api-6dbf78c7f9-zr8kd: Pod belongs to autoscaled workload Deployment/api

likely_causes: HPA behavior is too aggressive for this workload, especially if downscale stabilization is disabled or too short
Metrics near the target threshold are noisy enough that the HPA keeps alternating between scale-up and scale-down decisions
Pods take long enough to become Ready that the HPA reacts to transient startup utilization before capacity stabilizes
Manual or GitOps writes to spec.replicas are fighting with the autoscaler control loop
suggested_checks: kubectl get hpa api-hpa -n default -o yaml
Review HPA behavior.scaleDown stabilization and scaling policies for overly aggressive reversals
